kata-para-karate-examination-questions-d.text: World Karate Federation Kata and Para-Karate Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 1 / 8 Kata Competition Examination questions for Kata & Para - Karate Judges Version 1 st December 202 5 T he answer paper is to be returned to the examiners. All answers are to be entered on the separate answer paper only. You must make sure that your name , country, number and any other information required are entered on the answer paper. You may not have any additional papers or books on your desk while undertaking this examination. During the examination to be seen speaking to another candidate or copying another’s paper will mean suspension and automatic failure of the examination. If yo u are not sure of the correct procedures or have any questions about any aspect of the examination, you should speak only to an examiner , the RC Secretary or Asst. Secretary . The result of the examination both theory and practical will be sent to the candidate’s National Federation on request .

World Karate Federation Kata and Para-Karate Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 2 / 8 KATA & PARA - KARATE EXAMINATION “TRUE OR FALSE” On the answer paper put an “X” in the appropriate box. The answer to a question is true only if it can be held to be true in all situations; otherwise, it is considered to be false. Each correct answer scores one point. 1. Kata team members must all wear the same brand of Karate Gi. 2. The total time allowed for the Kata and BUNKAI demonstration combined, is six minutes. 3. In Kata competition slight variations as taught by the contestant's style ( Ryu - ha ) are permitted. 4. Glasses are forbidden in Kata com petition . 5. Only one discreet rubber band on a single ponytail is permitted. 6. The right to seeding does down flow to lower places in the absence of Athlete s eligible for seeding. 7. Kata competition can be organized in several formats . 8. In all competition format, t he number of Athlete s cannot exceed 8. 9. Judges should look for correct Kihon of the school being demonstrated. 10. Team matches consist of competition between teams of 3 or 4 Athlete s of which 3 compete at the time . 11. Conformance is a consistency in the performance of the K ihon of the style ( R yu - ha) in the Kata . 12. Should there be any discrepancy between the number and the name of the Kata registered for performance, the name, as per the official WKF Kata list will prevail . 13. When a team has 4 Athlete s, all 4 may be used for any round . 14. Giving a command to start and finish the Kata by a team member is not considered to be an external cue. 15. The mats must form a uniformed colo u red surface for Kata competition . 16. Judges should look for correct Kihon of the Athlete ´s style (Ryu - ha) being demonstrated.

World Karate Federation Kata and Para-Karate Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 3 / 8 17. The panel of Judges can be changed for any single round. 18. The total time allowed for the Kata & BUNKAI demonstration combined, is 5 minutes. 19. In Kata competition the sleeves of the Karate - gi jacket may not be rolled up . 20. It is the responsibility of the coach or Athlete to notify the name and the number of the Kata to the score table /runner prior each round. 21. The number of Judges per panel may be reduced to five for any competition. 22. A previously performed Kata may be repeated only if used as tiebreaker . 23. The Karate - G i trousers may be slightly shorter so as to only cover half of the shin . 24. The total time allowed for the Kata performance and Bunkai demonstration , is five minutes. 25. In the event that there are 3 or less Athlete s a single Kata is performed to determine 1st through 3rd place . 26. The Judges of a Kata bout may not have the same nationality as either of the contestants . 27. Although ribbons and other decorations for the hair are not allowed in kumite, they are permissible in Kata . 28. In the bouts for medals in Team Kata competition , teams will perform a demonstration of the meaning of the Kata ( BUNKAI ). 29. It is not possible to repeat a Kata in the Round - robin elimination system . 30. For competitions not counting for WKF World Ranking or Olympic standing, the number of judges may be reduced to 5. 31. The same Kata may never be performed twice in a row in the Round - robin system . 32. If an already qualified Team is disqualified for misconduct at the end of the Round - robin round (SHIKKAKU): The semi - final opponent will access to the final by bye . 33. Female Athlete s must wear a plain white T - shirt beneath the Karate jacket. 34. Female Athlete s can, if they want, wear a plain white T - shirt beneath the Karate jacket. 35. The wearing of unauthorised apparel, clothing or equipment is forbidden. 36. A discreet rubber band or ponytail retainer is permitted in Kata competition. 37. The matted Kumite areas are not suitable for Kata competition. 38. The Karate - gi jacket may be removed during the performance of Kata . 39. Audible cues are on the same level as one would penalize a temporary loss of balance. 40. Variations as taught by the contestant’s style (Ryu - ha) of Karate will not be permitted .

World Karate Federation Kata and Para-Karate Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 4 / 8 41. The score table must be notified of the choice of Kata prior to each round. 42. A team which does not perform the bow at the completion of the BUNKAI performance will be disqualified. 43. The number of Kata required is dependent on the number of individual Athlete s or teams entered. 44. If an already qualified Athlete is disqualified for misconduct at the end of the Round - robin round (SHIKKAKU): the bronze medal will be awarded . 45. In assessing the performance of a contestant or team the Judges will evaluate the performance based on all criteria. 46. There are no specific deviations for those under 14 years of age from the standard rules, but a limitation to the Kata list to less advanced Kata may be used . 47. Causing injury by lack of controlled technique during BUNKAI is considered a foul. 48. For medal bouts, none of the Judges may have the same nationality, as the Athlete s . 49. Performances are given a score using a scale from 5.0 to 10.0 in increments of 0.1 . 50. Coin toss is the first choice to resolve the ties . 51. There are 6 ways to resolve a tie In Round - robin Elimination System with 4 Athlete s . 52. Strength is one of the criteria of evaluation . 53. There are 6 ways to resolve a tie when having elimination system with 8 Athlete s . 54. There are 10 criteria for Kata evaluation . 55. Transitional movement is one of the Criteria for evaluation for Kata Performance but not for BUNKAI . 56. In BUNKAI performance, transitional movements and control are par t of the evaluation. 57. Strength, Speed and Balance are some of the criteria of evaluation for Kata and BUNKAI . 58. Simulated unconsciousness for more than 2 seconds at the time as part of the BUNKAI is considered as foul . 59. BUNKAI are to be given equal importance as the Kata itself. 60. Excessive bowing before starting the pe rformance is considered a foul. 61. Causing injury by lack of controlled technique during BUNKAI i s a disqualification . 62. Not announcing the Kata , is not considered as disqualification . 63. Performing the wrong Kata or announcing the wrong Kata leads to disqualification.

World Karate Federation Kata and Para-Karate Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 5 / 8 64. Stamping the feet, slapping the chest, arms, or Karate - gi, will be taken into account by the Judges when arriving at a decision. 65. A n Athlete will not be disqualified if he does a distinct pause or stop in the pe rformance for several seconds. 66. BUNKAI is not to be given equal imp ortance as the Kata itself. 67. Kata must start facing the Judges . 68. The use of audible cues from any other person, including other team members is c onsidered a foul. 69. If the Athlete ’s belt falls off during the performance of Kata, the Athlete will be disqualified. 70. Failure to follow the instructions of the Judge nr. 1 or other m isconduct is considered a foul. 71. Failure to follow the instructions of the Jud dge nr. 1 or other misconduct leads to disqualification. 72. Exceeding the total time limit of 5 minutes duration for Kata and BUNKAI is considered a foul. 73. In Team Kata , all three team members must start and finish the Kata facing in the same direction a nd towards the Judge s . 74. Kata performance must be realistic in fighting terms and display concentration, power, and pote ntial impact in its techniques. 75. Causing injury by lack of controlled technique during BUNKAI is not considered a foul. 76. Minor loss of balance must not be considered in the evaluation of the Kata performance. 77. Exc eeding the total time limit of 5 minutes duration for Kata and BUNKAI leads to disqualification. 78. Kata performance must demonstrate strength, balance, and speed. 79. Minor loss of balance must be considered in the evalua tion of the Kata performance. 80. Asynchronous movement, such as delivering a technique before the body transition is completed is considered a foul. 81. Inappropriate exhalation will be taken into account by the Judges when arriving at a decision. 82. In team Kata, failing to do a movement in unison is not considered a foul. 83. Performing a movement in an incorrect or incomplet e manner is considered a foul.

World Karate Federation Kata and Para-Karate Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 6 / 8 84. Time wasting, including prolonged marching, excessive bowing or prolonged pause before starting the performance is considered a foul. 85. Stamping the feet, slapping the chest, arms, or Karate - gi, are audible cues. 86. The use of audible cues is not considered a foul. 87. Inappropriate exhalation is not an audible cue. 88. Failure to fully execute a block or punching off target is considered a foul. 89. Causing injury by lack of controlled technique d uring BUNKAI is allowed. 90. The members of the team must demonstrate competence in all aspects of the Kata performance, as well as synchronisation. 91. Commands to start and stop the performance will be taken into account by the Judges when arriving at a decision. 92. It is the responsibility of the President of the National Federation to ensure that the Kata as notified to the score table is appropriate fo r that particular round. 93. While performing BUNKAI , after being downed the Athlete should either raise to one knee or stand up within 2 seconds . 94. Omitting or adding movements - or otherwise substantially change the performance from its original form is a disqualification . 95. Excessive celebration and political or religious demonstrations is considered as disqualification for kumite but not for Kata. 96. The Athlete must wait on the tatami for the announcement of the evaluation at the end of performance. 97. The Athlete s can select any Kata from the official Kata list. 98. A team which does not perform the bow at the beginning and completion of the performance wil l be disqualified. 99. The use of traditional weapons, ancillary equipm ent or additional apparel is allowed . 100. The performance in individual Kata is evaluated from the bo w starting the Kata until the b ow ending the Kata . 101. Belt coming loose to the extent that it is coming off the hip s during the performance is a foul. 102. Belt coming loose to the extent that it is coming off the hip s during the performance is a disqualification. 103. After the bow the Athlete must clearly announce the name of the Kata that is to be performed and then start the performance . 104. There are 9 criteria in each Kata evaluation .

World Karate Federation Kata and Para-Karate Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 7 / 8 105. Although performing a scissor takedown technique ( KANI BASAMI) to the neck area in performing BUNKAI is prohibited, a scissor take down to the body is permitted. 106. Performing a scissor takedown technique (KANI BASAMI) to the neck area in BUNKAI is not prohibited. 107. Audible cues must be considered very serious fouls by the judges in their evaluation of the performance of the Kata . 108. The protest can be submitted to a representative of the Appeals Jury by the coach . 109. It is the responsibility of the Tatami Manager receiving the protest to gather the Appeals Jury and deposit the protest sum with WKF for any declined protest . 110. Each of the three members of Appeal Jury is obliged to give hi s verdict as to the validity of the protest. Abstentions are not acceptable . 111. In Para - Karate , advertising i n the wheelchairs can be placed on the backrest. 112. If the protest is accepted, the Appeals Jury will appoint one of its members who will verbally notify the protester that the protest has been accepted . 113. There are three general Categories in Para Karate competitions: Visually Impaired , Intellectually Impaired and Physically Impaired . 114. In Para - Karate, p rostheses, canes, crutches, and other equipment used for physical support are allowed in Para - Karate competitions. 115. Those Athlete s whose disabilities have been classified as Ineligible or who have been considered non - compliant or in violation of competition rules are non - eligible to compete in WKF Para - Karate . 116. In Para - Karate, t he seven judges will give their scores based on the assessment of the Kata performance and the level of Athlete 's impairment . 117. Para - Karate competitions consist of both Kata and Kumite. 118. Wheelchairs with anti - tip wheels are allowed in Para - Karate competitions . 119. Classification in Para - Karate consists of an extra point compensation depending on the influence of the particular impairment when performing the Kata . 120. In Para - Karate, M edical Diagnostic Forms prove the eligibility of the Athlete ’s impairment and are, together with functional testing at the competition, a tool to provide a more accurate classification of the Athlete . 121. All Para - Karate Athlete s are allowed to wear white sport shoes . 122. In Para - Karate, i n case of additional Classification testing, Doping Control, fire or other need for emergency evacuation, the national coaches are directly responsible for safe removal of the Athlete s. 123. In Para - Karate, c ontestants from the Intellectually Impaired category may perform the same Kata in each round.

World Karate Federation Kata and Para-Karate Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 8 / 8 124. Service animals trained and certified to detect life - threatening conditions are not allowed at the external perimeter of the competition area in Para - Karate . 125. The Appeals Jury must elaborate a simple protest incident report, describing their findings and state their reason(s) for accepting or rejecting the protest . 126. There is 100 Kata on the official Kata list . 127. Resolution for ties in Para - Karate, will follow the WKF Kata Competition Rules. 128. In Para - Karate, t herapy animals or emotional - support animals are allowed in the competition area or its perimeter. 129. In Para - Karate, i t is allowed for Athlete s of Visually Impaired Classes to wear blindfolds with logos or markings from sponsors or manufacturers. 130. The straps to fix the legs of wheelchair Athlete s in Para - Karate, must be of white color and without logos. 131. In Para - Karate, sc ore compensation system by means of Extra Points, must be given by the judging panel to the influence of his or her particular impairment . 132. There are three Sport Classes in Individual Para - Karate Kata Competition . 133. Visually Impaired Athlete s in Para - Karate competition are divided in two categories . 134. Juniors/cadets Sport Classes are recommended for Para - Karate development . 135. In Para - Karate Competitions, b lindfolds are required for all Visually Impaired Athlete s during the performance . 136. Intellectual impair ed Athlete s , in Para - Karate, will perform the Kata standing up . 137. Intellectual impair ed Athlete s , in Para - Karate, can not repeat the Kata.

kumite-examination-questions-dec-2025.pd.text: World Karate Federation Kumite Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 1/17 Kumite Competition Examination Questions for Kumite Referees and Judges Version 1 st December 2025 The answer paper is to be returned to the examiners. All answers are to be entered on the separate answer paper only. You must make sure that your name, country, number and any other information required are entered on the answer paper. You may not have any additional papers or books on your desk while undertaking this examination. During the examination to be seen speaking to another candidate or copying another’s paper will mean suspension and automatic failure of the examination. If yo u are not sure of the correct procedures or have any questions about any aspect of the examination, you should speak only to an examiner, the RC Secretary or Asst. Secretary. The result of the examination s both theory and practical will be sent to the candidate’s National Federation on request .

World Karate Federation Kumite Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 2 / 17 KUMITE EXAMINATION “TRUE OR FALSE” On the answer paper put an “X” in the appropriate box. The answer to a question is true only if it can be held to be true in all situations ; otherwise, it is considered to be false. Each correct answer scores one point. 1. Any Voluntary religious headwear can be worn. 2. The K arate - Gi jacket must be more than three - quarters thigh length. 3. The K arate - Gi trousers must cover at least two - thirds of the shin. 4. The K arate - Gi jacket sleeve must be no longer than the bend of the wrist. 5. If a n Athlete ’s K arate - Gi jacket sleeves are too long and a suitable replacement cannot be found in time the Referee can allow him to turn them up on the inside. 6. Athlete s may wear 1 or 2 discreet rubber band or ponytail retainer in their hair. Ribbons, beads and other decorations are prohibited . 7. Earrings are allowed if they are covered with tape . 8. Metallic teeth (dental) braces may be worn at the Athlete ’s own risk if approved by the Referee and the Official Doctor. 9. Athlete s must bow properly to each other at the start and end of the bout . 10. The coach may change the team fighting order during a match . 11. If a n Athlete is injured in an individual bout the coach can enter a replacement if he notifies the Organising Commission first. 12. Each Judge must be equipped with a red and a blue flag or electronic signal device . 13. If two teams have the same number of victories and points, a deciding bout will be held. 14. The Match Supervisor (KANSA) will be equipped with only a whistle . 15. Disqualification by KIKEN means that the Athlete s are disqualified from that category . 16. Heels of more than 4 cm may not be worn with the uniform .

World Karate Federation Kumite Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 3 / 17 17. Senior Male bouts are three minutes, and Women, Cadets, and Juniors, two minutes. 18. Kumite team members may wear any type of Karate Gi. 19. Different type of Karate - Gi brands may be used for team members . 20. A n Athlete does not need to change the jacket if ties are torn off during the bout . 21. Athlete s may wear any white Karate Gi . 22. A fast combination of CHUDAN G eri and TSUKI each of which score in their own right will be given IPPON. 23. In S enior bouts a light “glo ve touch” to the throat need no result in a warning or penalty provided there is no actual injury. 24. A kick in the groin will not result in a penalty provided the offender did not do it intentionally. 25. Fo r Athlete s under 14 years of age , the WKF approved protective helmet is compulsory. 26. If SENSHU is wi thdrawn when it is less tha n 15 seconds left of the bout, no further SENSHU can be awarded to either Athlete . 27. Should a n Athlete that has been awarded SENSHU receive a warning for avoiding combat when there is less than 15 seconds left of the bout – the Athlete will automat ically forfeit this advantage. 28. Voluntary religious headwear must be approved by the WKF . 29. The WKF Competition Supervisor , or the Organizing Commission, can allow Coaches to instead of the track suit top to use any t - shirt . 30. HANSOKU - CHUI is given when the Athlete ’s potential for winning has been seriously reduced by the opponent’s foul. 31. The weight tolerance admitted for male and female 0.5 kg . 32. HANSOKU is imposed for very serious rules infractions. 33. SHIKKAKU can only be imposed after a warning has been given . 34. If a n Athlete acts maliciously, SHIKKAKU and not HANSOKU is the correct penalty. 35. If a n Athlete acts maliciously, H ANSOKU is the correct penalty. 36. A n Athlete can be given SHIKKAKU if the behaviour of the coach or non - combatant members of the Athlete ’s delegation is considered to harm the prestige and honour of Karate - do.

World Karate Federation Kumite Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 4 / 17 37. The same tolerance applies to both the upper and lower limits of a weight class . 38. For individual competition, the Round - robin system followed by quarterfinals, semi - finals and final is applied . 39. There are five criteria to be met in determining a score. 40. A male team must present minimum five Athlete s for the initial round . 41. Male teams’ elimination comprises five to seven members with five competing in a round . 42. A female team must present minimum two Athlete s for the initial round . 43. The maximum 32 Athlete s per category are divided in 8 groups of 4 Athlete s in the Round - robin system . 44. I f the coach continues to interfere after first warning, the Referee will stop the bout, approach the coach again and as k him/her to leave the tatami. 45. If AKA scores just as AO steps outside of the match area, both the score and warning or penalty can be given . 46. If a n Athlete has been physically propelled from the area, Jogai will be giv en . 47. In Premier League competition the losers to the finalists in the quarter - and semi - finals compete for the bronze medals . 48. In cases where there is a tie between two or more Athlete s in a group, having the same number of total points, the first criteria to consider is the highest World Ranking at the date of the competition . 49. It is possible for a n Athlete to be disqualified from a bout (HANSOKU) and continue the competition . 50. A n Athlete who scores and exits the area before the Referee calls YAME will not receive JOGAI . 51. Athlete cannot be given penalties after the time - up bell has signalled the end of a bout. 52. In Junior competition any technique to the face, head or neck, which causes injury will be warned or penalised unless it is the recipient’s own fault . 53. In Cadet and Junior competition Jodan kicks are allowed to make the lightest touch ( “skin touch” ) provided there is no injury. 54. In Senior competition a light touch is allowed for JODAN punches, and a greater tolerance is allowed for JODAN kicks.

World Karate Federation Kumite Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 5 / 17 55. An already qualified Athlete cannot be disqualified for misconduct (SHIKKAKU) at the end of the Round - robin . 56. If a variation of the competition format other than described in these rules is to be applied for a particular tournament, this has to be clearly announced in the tournament bulletin . 57. A b ad behaviour from a coach does not cause a S HIKKAKU to his/her Athlete and the Athlete does not need to be expelled from the match/bout. 58. When the Judges see a score, they will immediately signal with joy sticks or flags . 59. The referee panel is composed of 1 Tatami Manager, 3 Tatami Manager Assistants . 60. The Referee P anel for each bout shall consist of one Referee, four Judges and one Match Supervisor . 61. If after the bout has started a n Athlete is found not to be wearing a gum - shield the Athlete will be disqualified. 62. The Referee will give all commands and make all announcements. 63. Duration of the Kumite bout is: 1.5 minutes for under 14 years and younger . 64. If three Judges signal a score for AKA, the Referee must stop the bout even if he believes they are mistaken. 65. If two or more Judges signal a score for the same Athlete, the Referee must stop the bout. 66. The timing of a bout starts when the Referee gives the signal to start and stops when the Referee calls “ YAME ” or when the time is up. 67. The Referee P anel of a K umite bout shall consist of a Referee, four Judges, a Match Supervisor and a Score Keeper . 68. When a n Athlete slips and falls down on the floor with the torso not touching the Tatami and is immediately scored upon the opponent will be awarded Ippon . 69. The Athlete ’s coach is the only o nes allowed to make a protest. 70. If the Referee does not hear the time - up signal the Match Supervisor will blow the whistle. 71. The Athlete s must only bow properly to each other at the start of the bout . 72. In individual bouts a n Athlete who withdraws voluntarily from the bout is declared KIKEN and an extra eight points are awarded to the opponent.

World Karate Federation Kumite Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 6 / 17 73. Awareness is the state of continued commitment, which endures after the technique has landed. 74. If the organizer has a check - up of equipment before line - up, it is still KANSA´S responsibility to ensure that the equipment is in accordance with the rules . 75. A n Athlete who stays within the match area may score on a n Athlete outside the match area. 76. ATOSHI BARAKU means “ 15 seconds of bout time remaining”. 77. ATOSHI BARAKU means “1 0 seconds of bout time remaining”. 78. A “ skin touch” to the throat is allowed only in Senior competition. 79. If there are no points at the end of a bout in team matches the Referee will call for HANTEI. 80. Excessive contact after r epeated failure to block is a consideration for MUBOBI. 81. A n Athlete may be penalised for exaggeration even when there is an actual injury. 82. The Referee declare the winner; “AO (AKA) NO KACHI”, and if necessary, break the tie in case of HANTEI . 83. In the event of a tied score at the end of an inconclusive bout the Referee Panel (the Referee and the four Judges) will decide the bout by HANTEI . 84. When the Referee Panel has made a decision not in accordance with the Rules of Competition, the M atch Supervisor (KANSA) will immediately blow his whistle . 85. A n Athlete should not be penalized for being winded (loss of breath as a consequence of a technique) or simply reacting to an impact even if the technique merited a point by the opponent . 86. In team matches there is no e xtra bout . 87. Only the first correctly executed technique of a combination will score . 88. Athlete s that appear at the competition area with unauthorized equipment or irregular Karate - Gi will be given one minute to correct the attire . 89. Athlete s that are winded as a result of an impact should be allowed time to catch their brea th before the bout is resumed. 90. JODAN is described as the shoulders and the area above the collar bone . 91. IPPON is awarded for JODAN kicks or any techniques against an opponent whose any part of the body other than the feet is in contact with the TATAMI. 92. HANSOKU CHUI will be given for feigning of injury.

World Karate Federation Kumite Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 7 / 17 93. HANSOKU will be given for the first instance of exaggerating an injury. 94. A loss of breath by the recipient of a blow after receiving a CHUDAN technique does indicate lack of control . 95. A n Athlete can be given Hansoku directly for exaggerating the effects of injury . 96. A technique even if effective, delivered after an order to suspend or halt the bout shall not be scored an d may result in a penalty being imposed on the offender . 97. In Cadet Kumite JODAN kicks are allowed to make a “skin touch” provided there is no injury. 98. If there is an error in charting and the wrong Athlete s compete this cannot be changed afterwards. 99. An effective technique delivered at the same time as the end of the bout is signalled by the buzzer is valid. 100. If a n Athlete is injured and it is considered to be their own fault ( MUBOBI ) , the Referee will decline to give a warning or penalty to the opponent. 101. KANSA has no vote or authority in matters of judgments such as whether a score was valid or not . 102. YUKO is always awarded for punches on the back . 103. “Skin touch” is allowed in categories for Athlete s 16 years or older . 104. A n Athlete who does not obey the Referee's orders will be given SHIKKAKU . 105. The Score Supervisor will order the Referee to halt the bout when he sees a contravention of the Rules of Competition. 106. If in a combination, the first technique merits a YUKO and the second merits a penalty, both shall be given. 107. Skin touch is defined as touching the target without transferring energy into the head or body . 108. It is not possible to score while lying on the floor. 109. A n Athlete not wearing the WKF approved equipment will be given two minute s to change for the approved type. 110. A n Athlete injured in K umite and withdrawn under the ten - second rule may not compete in the K ata competition. 111. An injured Athlete who has been declared unfit to fight by the Tournament Doctor cannot fight again in that competition.

World Karate Federation Kumite Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 8 / 17 112. If a n Athlete behaves badly on the competition area after the end of the bout or bout, the Referee can still give SHIKKAKU . 113. The Match Supervisor has a vote in cases of SHIKKAKU . 114. In M ale team matches if a team wins three bouts, then the match is over at that point. 115. In F emale team matches if a team wins two bouts, then the match is over at that point. 116. When a n Athlete seizes the opponent and does not perform an immediate technique or throw the Referee will call “ YAME ”. 117. When a Judge is not sure that a techniqu e actually reached a scoring are a he may signal for the score. 118. A n Athlete who commits an act which harms the prestige and honour of Karate - D o will be given HANSOKU . 119. When an action of a n Athlete is considered to be dangerous and deliberately violates the rules concerning prohibited behaviour, the Athlete will be given Shikkaku. 120. Correctly executed techniques executed after the time - up signal are valid . 121. Correctly executed techniques executed upon or after “WAKARETE” are valid . 122. A point may be signaled even if the Judge cannot see the actual point of impact . 123. At HANTEI if three Judges signal victory for AKA and the fourth Judge signal s victory for AO , the Referee must give victory for AKA . 124. Passivity cannot be given to someone having a lead by point or SENSHU . 125. Only the Coach designated for the specific bout is allowed to coach and guide the Athlete from the place allocated to the coach close to the competition area . 126. If a n Athlete does not regain his or her feet within ten seconds, the Referee will announce “ KIKEN ” and “ KACHI ” to the opponent. 127. If a n Athlete is thrown and lands partly outside the match area, the Referee will immediately call “ YAME ”. 128. The Athlete may not signal to the Coach the wish for him/her to request a video review . 129. The Athlete should be examined outside the t atami.

World Karate Federation Kumite Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 9 / 17 130. KANSA has to blow the whistle if the Judge s are holding the joy stick s in the wrong hand s . 131. Gum - shields (mouth guards) are obligatory for all Kumite Athlete s . 132. After a throw the Referee will allow a maximum of two seconds for a score to be made. 133. If a n Athlete scores with a strong side kick and propels the opponent out of the area, the Referee should award WAZA - ARI and give the opponent a warning or penalty for Jogai. 134. When an official protest is lodged the ensuing bouts must be delayed until the result of the protest is given. 135. Each J udge will be seated in th e corners of the TATAMI and outside of the safety area . 136. When the Referee awards a point for a technique which has caused injury the M atch Supervisor should signal for the bout to be stopped. 137. When the Referee fails to hear the “time - up” bell the Scorekeeper should blow his whistle. 138. WAKARETE for breaking up a clinch can be given at any time of the bout . 139. When the Referee wants to give SHIKKAKU s he / he may call the Judges for a brief consultation. 140. When a n Athlete is injured during a bout in progress and needs medical treatment three minutes will be given in which to receive it, after that the Referee will decide whether the Athlete will be declared unfit to fight or whether more time will be given. 141. A n Athlete who exits the area ( JOGAI ) with less than 15 seconds of bout time remaining will be given a minimum of CHUI . 142. To correct a score given to the wrong Athlete the Referee should turn towards the Athlete given the score in error, make the sign of TORIMASEN , and then give the score to the opponent. 143. If a n Athlete scores with a well - controlled CHUDAN G eri, then accidentally punches his opponent in the face causing a slight injury, WAZA - ARI and a warning should be given. 144. When an injured Athlete has been given medical treatment and the O fficial D octor says that the Athlete can continue fighting the Referee cannot over - rule the D octor's decision. 145. The Referee can stop the bout even if the Judges do not signal.

World Karate Federation Kumite Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 10 / 17 146. Once WAKARETE is called by the Referee the Coaches do not have the opportunity to make a video request unless the techniques happened before WAKARETE was called. 147. “Avoiding Combat” refers to a situation where a n Athlete attempts to prevent the opponent from having the opportunity to scor e by using time wasting behaviour . 148. TSUZUKTETE, unless preceded by WAKARETE, is not used if there is less than 15 seconds left of the bout . 149. In t eam matches if two Athlete s injure each other and cannot continue and the points score is equal the R eferee will announce HIKIWAKE . 150. When there are less than 15 seconds of bout time remaining and a losing Athlete , desperately trying to equalise, exits the area ( JOGAI ) they will be given a minimum of HANSOKU - CHUI . 151. Techniques which land below the belt cannot score. 152. Techniques, which land on the shoulder blade, can score. 153. If AKA accidentally kicks AO on the hip and AO cannot continue the bout, then AO will be given KIKEN . 154. If a n Athlete is obviously out of breath due to lack of endu ra nce, t he Referee should stop the bout to give him time to recover. 155. A n Athlete who establishes a clear lead of eight points is declared the winner. 156. When time is up ; the Athlete who has scored the most points is declared the winner. 157. An otherwise correctly executed technique will be penalized if executed at the same time as WAKARETE has been called . 158. There are two degrees of official warnings . 159. CHUI is given, up to three times, for smaller infractions . 160. HANSOKU CHUI cannot be given if three CHUI has not already been given . 161. A warning or penalty for MUBOBI is only given when a n Athlete is hit or injured through his own fault or negligence. 162. A n Athlete who is hit through their own fault and exaggerates the effect should receive a warning or penalty for MUBOBI or exaggeration but not both. 163. If a n Athlete makes a good CHUDAN k ick and the opponent catches the leg a score cannot be given.

World Karate Federation Kumite Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 11 / 17 164. A n Athlete makes a JODAN kick with all six scoring criteria. The opponent puts his hand up to intercept the kick and the hand then lightly hits his own face ; the Referee can give IPPON since the kick was not effectively blocked. 165. A male team can compete with only two Athlete s. 166. Authorised advertising for WKF is displayed on the left sleeve of the K arate - G i. 167. National Federations are not allowed to put advertising on the Athlete ’s K arate - Gi . 168. A K umite Athlete who receives KIKEN cannot compete again in that tournament. 169. An extra bout is used only for team matches. 170. KANSA needs to blow the whistle if the Referee gives a score to a n Athlete and MUBOBI to the other. 171. Serious violation of conduct, discipline, or malicious behavior merits a HANSOKU CHUI . 172. In international competitions t he Referee must not have the same nationality as either of the Athlete s although one of the Judges can if both coaches agree. 173. The Match Supervisor (KANSA) will line up together with the Referee and the Judges. 174. The coaches will be seated outside the safety area, on their respective sides facing towards the official table. 175. KANSA does not need to interfere if the Referee gives a score to a n Athlete and exagg eration to the other. 176. In team matches the referee Panel will rotate for each bout - provided that all hold the required license. 177. In team matches the P anel will rotate for each bout only in bouts for medals. 178. The Ref eree can move about the entire t atami including the safety zones. 179. Female Athlete s must have WKF approved body protector female designed . 180. The red and blue belts must be without any personal embroideries or markings . 181. When a situation appears to possibly warrant a disqualification the Referee can call one or more of judges for a brief consultation ( FUKUSHIN SHUGO) before announcing any decision . 182. Athlete s must wear a white K arat e - Gi without personal embroider y. 183. Personal embroider y in the K arate - Gi is allowed only in bouts for medals.

World Karate Federation Kumite Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 12 / 17 184. Where contact is considered by the Referee to be too strong, but does not diminish the Athlete ’s chances of winning, a warning (CHUI) can be given . 185. Any technique, which results in injury, unless caused by the recipient cause a warning or penalty . 186. An obvious overreaction to a contact will receive a CHUI . 187. Judges cannot indicate a score or warning before the Referee stops the bout. 188. An obvious display of exaggeration will receive a HANSOKU . 189. Any instance of feigning an injury, whoever slight, will receive a minimum warning of CHUI . 190. An obvious display of any feigning such as staggering around, falling on the floor, standing up and falling down again, and so on, will receive SHIKKAKU directly . 191. Any feigning of an injury from a technique that in fact have been determined by the judges as a point will as a minimum result in HANSOKU CHUI . 192. If a n Athlete scores with more than one consecutive technique before YAME , the Judges must show the higher point regardless of which sequence the techniques scored . 193. In team matches, if after the extra bout there are no scores, or it is equal scores with no SENSHU , the bout will be decided by HANTEI . 194. JOGAI occurs when a n Athlete exits from the competition area, and it is not caused by the opponent. 195. The minimum warning for running away, avoiding combat or/and wasting time during ATOSHI BARAKU is HANSOKU CHUI . 196. The pivotal point of the throw must not be above the thrower’s hip level and the opponent must be held onto throughout, so that a safe landing can be made . 197. Holding on to the opponent’s Karate Gi is not permitted to break a fall . 198. KANSA does not need to interfere if the Referee gives a score for a technique done after the time is up. 199. YUKO is worth one point. 200. WAZA - ARI is worth two points . 201. IPPON is worth three points .

World Karate Federation Kumite Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 13 / 17 202. It is the duty of the Match Supervisor to ensure before each bout or bout that the Athlete s are wearing the approved equipment. 203. It is the duty of the Tatami Manager to ensure before each bout or bout that the Athlete s are wearing the approved equipment. 204. Coaches must present their accreditation together with that of their Athlete or team to the official table . 205. WAZA - ARI is awarded for CHUDAN Kicks. 206. IPPON is awarded for JODAN GERI and scoring techniques which are delivered on an opponent who has been thrown, has fallen o n their own accord, or is otherwise off their feet . 207. Individual bouts can not be declared a tie. 208. Passivity can be given to any Athlete at any time . 209. Passivity cannot be given during the first 15 seconds of a bout . 210. Simulated attacks with the head, knees, or elbows are offense s . 211. Avoiding combat during the last 15 seconds of the bout (ATO SHIBARAKU) will as a minimum result in HANSOKU CHUI and loss of SENSHU . 212. The Head Coach of a delegation can protest about a judgment to the members of the Refereeing Panel. 213. In a Kumite Tatami t wo mats are inverted with the red side turned up in a one meter distance from the mat centre to form a boundary between the Athlete s. 214. Jacket ties must be tied . 215. At the beginning of a bout Jackets without ties can be used. 216. In individual competition a n Athlete may be replaced by another after the drawing has taking place. 217. At medal bouts male coaches are required to wear a dark suit, shirt , tie and shoes covering the toes. 218. At medal bouts female Coaches may choose to wear a dress, pantsuit or a combination of jacket and skirt in dark colours and shoes covering the toes. 219. At medal bouts female coaches can not wear religious headwear. 220. A n Athlete that refuses following the instructions of the Referee or display a loss of temper will automatically receive HANSOKU . 221. Athlete s are not entitled to a period of time between bouts , equal to the standard duration time of the bout , for purpose of rest.

World Karate Federation Kumite Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 14 / 17 222. Passivity cannot be g iven after less than the last 15 seconds of the bout . 223. Any excessive celebration, such as falling on one’s knees etc., political, or religious expressions, during or immediately after the bout or match, are prohibited and may be subject to a fine equal to the protest fee determined by EC 224. If a n Athlete receives KIKEN, or SHIKKAKU, in Round - robin competition all previous bouts are voided from the result. 225. Grabbing the opponent’s arm or K arat e - GI with one hand is only allowed for attempting an i mmediately scoring technique or takedown. 226. Grabbing the opponen t with both hands is never allowed during the bout . 227. It is the Tatami Manager’s duty to appoint the video review Judge . 228. Should the number of IPPON and WAZA ARI be equal, the decision will be made by HANTEI . 229. In any individual bout, with equal score, and no SENSHU by either Athlete , the first criteria of the decision will be made based on the higher number of IPPON scored in the bout . 230. If one Athlete has a score indicated by more than one Judge and the score is different between the Judges, the higher will be applied , unless there is majority for a lower score. 231. The Tatami Manager must notify the central table when a n Athlete has been stopped from further competition based on the 10 - second rule . 232. The timekeeper is responsible for starting the 3 - minute count for a n Athlete who is injured during a bout in progress and requires medical treatment. 233. The Referee will not call YAME when a n Athlete seizes the opponent and does not perform an immediate technique or throw. 234. The Referee indicates the winner by hand signal and the order (AKA/AO NO KACHI), and by this action also resolve any tie . 235. When a n Athlete seizes the opponent, the Referee will give several seconds for the Athlete to perform a throw or technique. 236. The Coach / NF representative will request the official protest from the Tatami Manager . 237. The Referee can stop the bout and give a point without the Judges opinion.

World Karate Federation Kumite Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 15 / 17 238. If the referee does not call the doctor in a 10 second rule situation, Kansa must blow the w h istle . 239. The winning team is the one with the most bout victori es excluding those won by SENSHU . 240. The red and blue belts must be no longer than three - quarters thigh length. 241. Failure of a Coach / NF representative to deliver a protest in a timely manner can lead to its rejection . 242. The correct penalty for feigning an injury when the judges have determined that the technique in fact was a score is HANSOKU . 243. The information of officials implicated in the protest is completed by the Tatami M anager . 244. Disqualification by KIKEN means that the Athlete is disqualified from that category, although it does not affect participation in another category. 245. Athlete s are entitled to a rest period of time between bout s, equal to the standard duration time of the bout . The exception is in the case of change of equipment color, where this time is extended to five minutes. 246. In any bout, if after full time the scores are equal, b ut one Athlete has obtained “ first un opposed score advantage” ( SENSHU ), that Athlete will be declared the winner. 247. By “ first un opposed score advantage” ( SENSHU ) is understood that one Athlete has achieved the first instance of scoring on the opponent without having the opponent also score before the signal. 248. Where both Athlete s score before the signal, no “first unopposed score advantage” is awarded and both Athlete s retain the possibility of SENSHU later in the bout. 249. In the event that a n Athlete falls, is thrown, or knocked down and does not regain his or her feet immediately, the Referee will call the doctor, and at the same time start a count to ten indicating his count showing a finger for each second. 250. An audible signal will be given by the timekeeper 15 seconds before the actual end of the bout and the Referee will announce “ ATOSHI BARAKU”. 251. Kansa must remain silent if the referee gives a warning or penalty for p assivity during ATOSHI BARAKU. 252. In individu al categories with elimination format, a bout can be declared a tie. 253. For all competition formats the Coach will be allowed one video review card for the Athlete .

World Karate Federation Kumite Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 16 / 17 254. For Round - robin in groups of four the Coach will be allowed one VR card for each participant in the round - robin phase. 255. The Athlete may ask the Coach to request a video review discretely. 256. The video review can be requested by the Coach in instances where the Judges awarded a lower score than, in the opinion of the Coach, should be for a higher scoring technique. 257. The Video Review Judge may only award points if scoring before or simultaneously with the other Athlete . 258. The last 6 seconds before the bout was stopped for the request will always be evaluated for Video Review. 259. If both Coaches request video review at the same time, the Video review Judge may only award the point to whoever is deemed to score first. 260. Simultaneous scoring techniques can be awarded to both Athlete s in Video Review. 261. The Video Review Judge may not overrule any decision made by the corner judges with the exception of SENSHU. 262. Tatami manager must supervise that KANSA stops the bout to instruct the Referee regarding a contravention of the Rules of Competition. 263. Referee indicates fouls observed, and imposes warning and penalties as required by the rules. 264. Referee breaks the tie in case of HANTEI. 265. it is always KANSA´ S responsibility to ensure that the equipment is in accordance with the rules before each bout . 266. In the event that the Referee does not hear the time - up bell, KANSA will blow his whistle. 267. In two judging systems , the Judges will assist the Referee by giving signals for JOGAI, excessive contact, and skin touch for categories where this contravenes the rules. 268. In two judging system s , the Referee remains autonomous in applying warnings and penalties.

World Karate Federation Kumite Examination Questions. Version 1 st December 2025 17 / 17 269. In two judging systems, if the two Judges, or one Judge and the Referee, show different points for the same Athlete , the higher will be given.

official-protest-guidelines-2025.pdf.text: WORLD KARATE FEDERATION HQ Office: Calle Princesa 25, 3º 1 - 28008 Madrid – Spain, Tel. +34 91 535 96 32, e-mail: wkf@wkf.net www.wkf.net 1 0 th October 202 5 GUIDELINES FOR HANDLING AN OFFICIAL PROTEST AT WKF EVENTS These general guidelines for handling an official protest are to prepare Judges and Referees for resolving protest situations in the most correct and efficient way, taking into consideration that Fair Play in competition is essential to guarantee the correct outcome of a Kumite bout or Kata performance. Terminology Matters of Judgement (Decisions) are based upon the personal perception/observation of an actual situation by the Referee Panel and are not grounds for protest . Matters of Procedure (Violations of the Rules) by the Referee Panel or any other administrative person involved in the bout, which means an incorrect application of the rules. Correction of decisions In the WKF Rules of competition, the possibility of retroactive correction of decisions is stipulated if the Referee Panel or any other administrative person involved in the bout violated the rules. The WKF Competition Rules allow for two control mechanisms against the violation of the rules by the Referee Panel: • The Match Supervisor (Kansa) or Tatami Manager (TM) • The possibility of an Official Protest. This ensures that during a competition, because of the control exercised by the Match Supervisor (KANSA) and Tatami Manager the number of official protests is limited. It also establishes a very clear and transparent principle, which can be applied regularly in every WKF Official competition. Note An official protest can include several situations/decisions taken by the Ref. Panel during the same bout. The Appeals Jury will investigate and decide separately on each of the complaints included in the protest.

WORLD KARATE FEDERATION HQ Office: Calle Princesa 25, 3º 1 - 28008 Madrid – Spain, Tel. +34 91 535 96 32, e-mail: wkf@wkf.net www.wkf.net The following cases o f Matter of Judgment are examples when a protest cannot be accepted by the Appeal Jury for discussion and decision because it is a matter of judgment. • If the Referee gives a score but in the opinion of the Coach the technique has not fulfilled all six scoring criteria. • If the Referee gives a score but in the opinion of the Coach the technique has missed the target area. • If the Referee gives a score but in the opinion of the Coach the technique was blocked. • If the Referee gives a contact, but in the opinion of the Coach, it was MUBOBI (or the other way around). The following cases o f Matter of Procedures are examples o f when a protest can be accepted by the Appeal Jury for discussion and decision: 1. The Referee forgot to indicate SENSHU. 2. The Referee forgot to remove SENSHU. 3. The Referee gave a score to the wrong competitor. 4. The Referee gave a warning/penalty to the wrong competitor. 5. The Referee gave a score to a competitor and a warning for exaggeration to the opponent. 6. The Referee gave a score to a competitor and MUBOBI to the opponent. 7. The Referee gave contact to a competitor and MUBOBI to the opponent. 8. The Referee gave a score for a technique done after WAKARETE and before TSUZUKETE. 9. The Referee gave a score for a technique done at the same time as WAKARETE. 10. The Referee gave a score for a technique that caused an injury. 11. The Referee gave a wrong level of score (i.e. YUKO instead of WAZA - ARI for CHUDAN GERI, or YUKO instead of IPPON when another part of the body than the opponent’s feet was in contact with the mat). 12. The Referee gave a warning or penalty for passivity during ATO SHIBARAKU. 13. The Referee gave the wrong warning (i.e., passes from 0 directly to CHUI 2 or 3).

WORLD KARATE FEDERATION HQ Office: Calle Princesa 25, 3º 1 - 28008 Madrid – Spain, Tel. +34 91 535 96 32, e-mail: wkf@wkf.net www.wkf.net 14. The Referee gave the wrong warning during ATO SHIBARAKU. 15. The Referee didn’t stop the bout, if two or more judges signal a score for the same competitor. 16. The Referee didn’t stop the bout in case of a Video Review request by a Coach. 17. A score is awarded in Video Review, but the opponent scored before. 18. The Referee didn’t follow the majority of scores signaled by the judges. 19. The Referee didn’t follow the majority of Judges when deciding HANTEI. 20. The Referee gave the lower score when the level of score is different between the Judges. 21. The Referee allowed the bout to continue when a competitor is declared unfit to fight by the Tournament Doctor. 22. The Referee did HANTEI/HIKIWAKE, but SENSHU has been obtained. 23. The Referee did HANTEI, but the competition format (regular team match/round robin) requires HIKIWAKE. 24. The Referee did HANTEI/HIKIWAKE when the score is equal without any Competitor having SENSHU and one of Competitors has the higher number of IPPON and/or WAZA - ARI. 25. The score board was not showing the correct information. 26. The team’s composition or fighting order was changed before or during the match. PROCEDURE WHEN AN OFFICIAL PROTEST IS PRESENTED As soon as the Tatami Manager gives the protest form to the protester: a. Inform the Appeals Jury Supervisor. b. Collect Appeals Jury nr.1. c. To avoid conflicts of interest, the TM informs Appeals Jury nr.1 of the people involved. d. Appeals Jury nr.1 collect the rest of the AJ members needed.

WORLD KARATE FEDERATION HQ Office: Calle Princesa 25, 3º 1 - 28008 Madrid – Spain, Tel. +34 91 535 96 32, e-mail: wkf@wkf.net www.wkf.net When the protest has been paid by the protester and completed by the Tatami Manager, the Appeals Jury will first note the time and then begin checking the evidence by using the following alternatives: a. Interviewing the Coach/protester. b. Watching/ analyzing a video, photo, or any other significant evidence. c. If necessary, watching/ analyzing the Video Review recordings. d. Talking with the Referee Panel, Tatami Manager, or any other relevant witness. When the protest has been evaluated: a. The Appeals Jury decides to accept or decline the protest. b. All three members must vote, and the majority will apply. I f the protest is declined: a. After written “Decline”, all three Appeals Jury Members sign the rejected protest form. b. The Appeals Jury appoints a Member to verbally notify the protester, the Tatami Manager, the Chief Referee and the Local Organizer Commission (LOC). c. After that, the Appeals Jury meets again and writes the incident report. d. The protest form together with the incident report and the protest fee is given by the AJ Supervisor to the LOC or in the case of a Karate 1 Event to the WKF Supervisor of the Event. e. An extra copy of the protest and incident report is given to the Chief Referee of the event.

WORLD KARATE FEDERATION HQ Office: Calle Princesa 25, 3º 1 - 28008 Madrid – Spain, Tel. +34 91 535 96 32, e-mail: wkf@wkf.net www.wkf.net I f the protest is accepted: a. After written “Accepted”, all three Appeals Jury Members sign the accepted protest form. b. The Appeals Jury appoints a Member to verbally notify the protester and then liaise with the TM, the Chief Referee and LOC to take the measures needed for remedy the situation. c. The protest fee is returned to the Protester by the AJ Supervisor. d. After that, the Appeals Jury meets again and writes the incident report. e. The protest form together with the incident report are given by the AJ Supervisor to the LOC or in the case of a Karate 1 Event to the WKF Supervisor of the Event. f. An extra copy of the protest and the incident report is given to the Chief Referee of the event. An accepted protest does not mean that a bout or match will automatically be redone. In order to correct the situation, the Appeals Jury has the following possibilities: 1. Reversing previous judgments that contravene the rules. 2. Voiding results of the affected rounds from the score before the incident. 3. Redoing such bouts that have been affected by the incident. 4. Issuing a recommendation to the WKF President and RC Chairperson for any involved Referees/Judges evaluated for sanction.

referee-conditions-2026.pdf.text: HQ Office: Calle Núñez de Balboa 120, 7º izda. - 28006 Madrid – Spain, Tel. +34 91 535 96 32, e-mail: wkf@wkf.net www.wkf.net WORLD KARATE FEDERATION Issued December 2025 CONDITIONS AND REQUIREMENTS FOR REFEREES 202 6 1. KARATE WORLD CHAMPIONSHIPS World Cadet, Junior & U21 Championships 2026 • License requirements: Referees with minimum Kumite Judge B and/or Kata Judge B license. • Annual * attendance requirements for registering as a Referee: Minimum participation requirements in K1 events for an aggregate of four (4) K1 events, with a minimum of: o 1 Series A o 2 Youth League o 1 K1 event at free choice in at least 2 Continents. • R egistration : o The appointment of Referees will be done directly by the NFs. o The appointment of Referees by the NFs will not be limited to a maximum number. • A Referee Course for qualified Referees will take place ( see 3. for details ). Teams World Cup 2026 • License requirements: Referees with highest licen s es Kumite Referee A and Kata Judge A license. • Annual * attendance requirements for registering as a Referee: o 1 Premier League For the members of the RC and those wishing to be eligible for the list of WKF appointed referees, the minimum requirement will be of 2 Premier Leagues. o 1 Series A o 2 Youth League

HQ Office: Calle Núñez de Balboa 120, 7º izda. - 28006 Madrid – Spain, Tel. +34 91 535 96 32, e-mail: wkf@wkf.net www.wkf.net WORLD KARATE FEDERATION i n at least 3 Continents. • Registration: o WKF RC members (see Annex 1) o Referees appointed by WKF (see Annex 1) o NFs will be allowed to register a maximum of two (2) referees per NF that comply with the overall established annual requirements, regardless of those nationalities already included with the RC Members and those Referees appointed by the WKF * Note: The term "annual" refers to the time span starting from (and including) the first K1 tournament after the World Championships until (and including) the last K1 tournament before the next year's World Championship. 2. KARATE 1 EVENTS O nline registration of Referees will be structured in separate registrations as follows: For Premier League events: • Referees with a WKF License: limited to 75 (the WKF reserves its right to stop the registration once it has reached the number of 75 ) • Referee Commission members • Referees ranked within the top 30 of the WKF Referee Ranking on the opening date of registration for each event. • Note: Only Referees with a WKF Kata Judge - A and/or Kumite Referee - A License are entitled to register for Premier League events and to officiate in the respective category/categories, where they hold the highest license. For Series A and Youth League events: • The WKF reserves its right to stop the registration once the number of registered referees with WKF and /or Continental Licens e has reached the number of: ▪ 90 in Series A ▪ 120 in Youth League. • Referee Commission members • Referees ranked within the top 30 of the WKF Referee Ranking on the opening date of registration for each event. • NF Referees without WKF and/or Continental License General T aking into account the overall situation and adequate conditions for a successful event at the highest level , special requests may be considered on a case - by - case basis after the closing of the registration .

HQ Office: Calle Núñez de Balboa 120, 7º izda. - 28006 Madrid – Spain, Tel. +34 91 535 96 32, e-mail: wkf@wkf.net www.wkf.net WORLD KARATE FEDERATION 3. WKF REFEREE COURSES There will continue to be two (2) separate Referee Courses per year, that will be included in the WKF Calendar: Course for Referees at a Karate1 E vent a. Referees without WKF license b. Referees who have their WKF license expired in the previous year The event at which the course will be held, shall serve as confirmation event. This annual Referee Course will take place, preferably between February and July and preferably at the occasion of a YL event. Course for Referees at World Championships (World Championships for Cadets, Juniors & U21 in 202 6 ) a. Referees already having a WKF license b. Referees who have their WKF license expired in the previous year The World Championships at which the course will be held shall serve as confirmation event. 4. REQUIREMENTS FOR THE WKF LICENCE RENEWAL • Participate annually in at least one Karate1 event • Participate in at least one World Championships during the period of validity of the license • Pass the written License Renewal exam every 4 (four) years Those who do not comply with the above will have to attend and pass the examination at a WKF Referee Course in Kata and/or Kumite to reinstate the license(s). After a year has passed without having a valid license, it is necessary to attend a WKF Referee C ourse and begin from the beginning. • Payment of License Renewal (PLR) needs to be done every 4 (four) years

HQ Office: Calle Núñez de Balboa 120, 7º izda. - 28006 Madrid – Spain, Tel. +34 91 535 96 32, e-mail: wkf@wkf.net www.wkf.net WORLD KARATE FEDERATION ANNEX 1 Methodology for the selection by the WKF of the Officials for refereeing in the Teams World Cup 2026 The WKF will assume the flight ticket and the full board accommodation in the HQ hotel for the full RC Commission. In addition to the RC members, WKF will appoint a maximum of 30 referees complying with the established requirements and will assume for each of them a benchmark lump sum for the flight ticket as well as the full board accommodation in one of the official hotels. Requirements: 1. Highest WKF license Kumite Referee - A and Kata Judge - A The full Referee Commission, plus a ll selected referees must be WKF qualified with the highest licenses in Kata and Kumite. 2. Compliance with the Referees’ requirements for attending a WKF World Championships, including one extra Premier League The selected referees must have attended on the relevant year a minimum of two (2) Premier Leagues, two (2) Youth Leagues and one (1) Series - A, in at least three (3) continents. 3. Activity attendance according to the WKF Referee Ranking The r eferees will be selected among the list of the 70 first in the Referees Ranking, tak ing into consideration the breakdown between Continental Federations as per point 4 below and compliant with point 2 above. 4. Universality (Continental Federations) The breakdown of the Referees between Continental Federations and maximum numbers by continent will be as follows: EKF: 1 1 Officials (40%) AKF: 7 Officials (~25%) UFAK: 5 Officials (~15%) PKF: 5 Officials (~15%) OKF: 2 Official s (~5%) Methodology for the selection of the referees The preliminary selection of the referees will be made within a deadline of around five ( 5 ) months before the 2026 Teams World Cup . Those selected will receive an appointment letter from the WKF and will have to answer accepting the appointment and the conditions associated, including the commitment to fulfil the requirements in place - as per point 2 above - before the date of the Championships.

HQ Office: Calle Núñez de Balboa 120, 7º izda. - 28006 Madrid – Spain, Tel. +34 91 535 96 32, e-mail: wkf@wkf.net www.wkf.net WORLD KARATE FEDERATION The selection will be made by taking the number of referees specified in point 4 before for each of the 5 Continents, continent by continent, using the top 70 ( seventy ) referees in the ranking list by the date of the selection. There will be no limitation in the number of referees selected for specific citizenship. Those referees initially selected that for whatever reasons excuse themselves from attending the World Championships will be replaced by another one in the ranking from the same Continent within the top 70 . When the substitution line is exhausted, then the replacement will be made using the Ranking list irrespective of the Continent, starting with the best ranked and following the same path within the top 70 . Once the selection of the Referees has been made, and the confirmations have been received, the NFs will be informed of the names of the ir respective Referees on the list, in order to be able to make their respective appointment of the up to two (2) referee s there are entitled to appoint per NF.
